exploreCEHR 0.0.2
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Resource use in training

exploreCEHR: overview of EHR-oriented transformer from Pang et al. 2024 arXiv:2402.04400

Vincent J. Carey, stvjc at channing.harvard.edu

April 03, 2026

Source: vignettes/exploreCEHR.Rmd
exploreCEHR.Rmd

Introduction

The purpose of this R package is to survey some of the resources used and produced in the operation of an autoregressive transformer system for predicting health events based on electronic health records.

A dialogue with Claude Sonnet 4.6 extended led to isolation of a data simulation process and some exploratory training. A key objective is estimation of resource requirements for creating similar models de novo.

The flavor of the information to be processed is sketched here.

Vocabulary for health event series

A “vocabulary” is defined. Some semantics of annotations can be gleaned from comments in the simulation program:

simulate_cehr_gpt.py
====================
Generate 100 K realistic longitudinal health event trajectories in the
CEHR-GPT token-sequence format.

Token-sequence schema (per patient)
-------------------------------------
  START_YEAR:YYYY  AGE:XX  GENDER:M/F  RACE:<race>
  [VS] [VT:OP|IP|ED]  concept…  [VE]
  [ATT:D<n>]      <- inter-visit gap (n = 1-1080 days)
  [ATT:LT]        <- gap > 1080 days
  ...
  [EOS]

Inpatient visits additionally contain intra-visit day tokens [IATT:D<n>]
and end with a discharge-facility token [DC:<facility>].

Outputs  (./output/)
---------------------
  sequences.parquet  -- one row per patient; full token sequence as string
  events.parquet     -- MEDS-style long table (subject_id | time | code | domain)
  summary_stats.json -- prevalence, visit counts, sequence-length stats

browse_notation provides additional details:

library(exploreCEHR)
browse_notation()

Elementary tabulation of event series

Patient’s event histories are tabulated. The table in this vignette is limited to 5000 records; 8034223 simulated records are available.

browse_events()

Data structure for modeling

Event sequences are reformatted to per-patient records.

In the following we break up the string for one patient’s visits to isolate a single visit and the prescriptions and conditions noted.

example(view_sequences)
## 
## vw_sqn> tt = view_sequences()
## 
## vw_sqn> strsplit(tt[[1]][[2]], " ")
## [[1]]
##  [1] "[VS]"             "[VT:OP]"          "G47.33"           "E11.9"           
##  [5] "I50.9"            "E66.9"            "M10.9"            "PR:abi"          
##  [9] "E03.9"            "PR:lipid_panel"   "PR:bmp"           "RX:aspirin"      
## [13] "PR:ekg"           "RX:sitagliptin"   "RX:levothyroxine" "I73.9"           
## [17] "PR:echo"          "RX:glipizide"     "RX:allopurinol"   "RX:metformin"    
## [21] "RX:semaglutide"   "I63.9"            "N18.4"            "K57.30"          
## [25] "RX:warfarin"      "E78.5"            "I48.91"           "RX:ezetimibe"    
## [29] "K76.0"            "E11.40"           "I10"              "RX:apixaban"     
## [33] "RX:losartan"      "RX:atorvastatin"  "RX:empagliflozin" "I25.10"          
## [37] "J18.9"            "[VE]"             "[ATT:D21]"

Developed by Vince Carey, Claude Sonnet4.6ext.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
